papers.title: Geometry-Aware Representation Denoising for Robust Multi-view 3D Reconstruction
papers.abstract: Abstract A novel diffusion-based framework for multi-view 3D reconstruction that restores both scene geometry and high-quality imagery from degraded inputs by operating in the feature space of a 3D reconstructor.  AI-generated summary Multi-view 3D reconstruction has achieved remarkable progress with the advent of feed-forward 3D reconstruction models. However, these models are typically trained and evaluated under ideal, degradation-free imaging conditions, whereas real-world observations often contain degradations that differ significantly from such settings. Improving robustness for multi-view 3D reconstruction under degraded conditions therefore remains an important challenge. We present Geometry-Aware Representation Denoising (GARD), a novel framework that performs diffusion-based multi-view restoration directly in the feature space of a feed-forward 3D reconstruction model. This design exploits the geometry-aware feature representations of the 3D reconstructor to effectively recover accurate scene geometry. Furthermore, by employing an additional RGB image decoder, the refined representations can also be used to restore high-quality RGB images, thereby enabling the simultaneous recovery of 3D scene geometry and high-quality imagery. Comprehensive experiments on the Depth Anything 3 (DA3) benchmark demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed GARD framework.

ru.title: Восстановление 3D геометрии и изображений из испорченных данных через диффузионное шумоподавление
ru.desc: В статье предложена новая структура на основе диффузионных моделей для восстановления многовидовой 3D реконструкции из деградированных входных данных. Метод работает в пространстве признаков предварительно обученной нейронной сети 3D реконструктора, эксплуатируя её геометрически-осведомленные представления. Система использует дополнительный декодер RGB для одновременного восстановления как геометрии сцены, так и высококачественных изображений. Эксперименты на эталонном наборе Depth Anything 3 подтверждают эффективность предложенного подхода GARD.

en.title: Reviving 3D Scenes: GARD for Robust Reconstruction
en.desc: This paper introduces a new method called Geometry-Aware Representation Denoising (GARD) for improving multi-view 3D reconstruction. It focuses on restoring both the shape of a scene and high-quality images from degraded inputs by working in the feature space of a 3D model. The GARD framework uses advanced diffusion techniques to enhance the geometry-aware features, allowing for better recovery of scene details. Experiments show that GARD effectively addresses challenges posed by real-world image degradations, achieving impressive results in 3D reconstruction and image quality.

zh.title: 基于扩散的多视角三维重建新框架
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种新颖的基于扩散的多视角三维重建框架，旨在从退化输入中恢复场景几何和高质量图像。该框架在三维重建模型的特征空间中进行操作，利用几何感知特征表示来有效恢复准确的场景几何。通过引入额外的RGB图像解码器，改进后的表示还可以用于恢复高质量的RGB图像，从而实现三维场景几何和高质量图像的同时恢复。实验结果表明，所提出的GARD框架在Depth Anything 3基准测试中表现出色。

papers.title: EvalVerse: Pipeline-Aware and Expert-Calibrated Benchmarking for Professional Cinematic Video Generation
papers.abstract: Abstract EvalVerse presents a comprehensive evaluation framework for generative video models that bridges the gap between human aesthetic judgment and machine scoring through expert-calibrated vision-language models and multi-stage cinematic assessment.  AI-generated summary The rapid evolution of generative video foundation models has propelled the field toward professional-grade cinematic synthesis. To achieve such demanding quality, the community transitions towards Reinforcement Learning (RL) and agentic workflows. However, reliable evaluation has emerged as a critical bottleneck. Existing benchmarks predominantly evaluate ''whether it is right'' (basic prompt-following) while fundamentally neglecting ''whether it is good'' (cinematic quality, acting, and aesthetics). Furthermore, current automated metrics lack the domain-specific rigor required to provide trustworthy signals, creating a severe credibility gap between human aesthetic perception and machine scoring. To bridge this gap, we introduce EvalVerse, a comprehensive, pipeline-aware, and expert-calibrated evaluation framework. We treat video generation assessment not merely as an engineering task, but as a core scientific problem: the systematic digitization of subjective cinematic expertise. First, we organize domain knowledge into an evaluation taxonomy aligned with the professional filmmaking workflow (pre-production, production, and post-production). Second, we distill human expert judgments into a curated dataset with large-scale human annotations. Third, we inject this knowledge into Vision-Language Models (VLMs) through an expert-calibrated fine-tuning strategy, enabling the VLM to perform explicit Chain-of-Thought reasoning. Compared to previous works, EvalVerse not only retains compatibility with foundational ''rightness'' metrics, but also significantly expands the criteria to ''goodness'' and broaden the task coverage to complex multi-shot sequencing and audio-visual integration. Consequently, by providing granular diagnostic signals, EvalVerse transcends a static leaderboard and establishes a fundamental infrastructure for future work, such as reward models and evaluator agent.
affiliations: Institute of Automation, Chinese Academy of Sciences
The Chinese University of Hong Kong
The Hong Kong University of Science and Technology

ru.title: Эксперты и машины: оценка кинематографического качества видео через калибровку знаний
ru.desc: EvalVerse представляет комплексную систему оценки для моделей генеративного видео, которая объединяет эстетическое суждение человека и автоматические метрики через калибровку экспертами. Авторы организуют знания в область видеопроизводства, используя профессиональную таксономию кинематографического рабочего процесса (подготовка, съёмка и постпродакшн). Они применяют стратегию тонкой настройки Vision-Language Model на основе экспертных аннотаций для выполнения цепочки рассуждений при оценке качества видео. Фреймворк не только сохраняет совместимость с базовыми метриками правильности, но и расширяет критерии на оценку эстетического качества, включая многокадровое монтирование и аудиовизуальную интеграцию.

en.title: Bridging Human Aesthetics and Machine Evaluation in Video Generation
en.desc: EvalVerse is a new evaluation framework designed for generative video models that combines human aesthetic judgment with machine scoring. It addresses the limitations of existing benchmarks that focus only on basic prompt-following, by incorporating a comprehensive assessment of cinematic quality, acting, and aesthetics. The framework organizes filmmaking knowledge into a structured evaluation process and uses expert-calibrated Vision-Language Models to enhance the evaluation of video generation. By expanding the criteria for assessment, EvalVerse aims to provide more reliable and nuanced feedback for future developments in video generation technology.

zh.title: EvalVerse：弥合人类审美与机器评分的桥梁
zh.desc: EvalVerse是一个全面的评估框架，旨在为生成视频模型提供评估，弥合人类审美判断与机器评分之间的差距。该框架通过专家校准的视觉-语言模型和多阶段电影评估，系统化地数字化主观电影专业知识。EvalVerse不仅关注生成视频的基本正确性，还扩展到评估其美学质量和复杂的多镜头序列。通过提供细致的诊断信号，EvalVerse为未来的奖励模型和评估代理建立了基础设施。

papers.title: LongAV-Compass: Towards Unified Evaluation of Minute-Scale Audio-Visual Generation Across T2AV, I2AV, and V2AV
papers.abstract: Abstract LongAV-Compass is a comprehensive benchmark for evaluating minute-long audio-visual generation across multiple modalities, assessing quality, consistency, and alignment over extended temporal sequences.  AI-generated summary Audio-visual generation is rapidly advancing from short clips to minute-long content, while existing evaluation protocols remain largely confined to short-form settings. Existing benchmarks primarily focus on 5--10 second text-conditioned generation and rarely support unified evaluation across text, image, and video conditioning modalities. Moreover, they provide limited insight into how identity consistency, narrative coherence, and audio-visual alignment degrade over extended temporal horizons. To bridge this gap, we introduce LongAV-Compass, a systematic benchmark for minute-long audio-visual generation. LongAV-Compass contains 284 curated test cases spanning text-to-audio-video (T2AV), image-to-audio-video (I2AV), and video-to-audio-video (V2AV), organized by application scenario and generation complexity. The benchmark combines taxonomy-guided benchmark construction with a unified evaluation framework that integrates MLLM-assisted assessment with complementary perceptual and multimodal metrics, including DINO-v2, ArcFace, CLIP, and ImageBind. The framework evaluates more than 20 fine-grained dimensions covering within-segment quality, cross-segment consistency, global narrative coherence, semantic alignment, and audio-visual synchronization. Through experiments on 11 representative models together with human-alignment validation, LongAV-Compass provides a diagnostic testbed for analyzing the limitations of current systems in sustaining coherent, semantically aligned, and temporally consistent minute-scale audio-visual generation across diverse input modalities.

ru.title: Компас для оценки минутных аудиовизуальных генераций
ru.desc: LongAV-Compass — это комплексный бенчмарк для оценки генерации аудиовизуального контента длительностью в минуту по нескольким модальностям. В отличие от существующих методов, сосредоточенных на коротких видеороликах (5-10 секунд), этот бенчмарк поддерживает единую оценку генерации из текста, изображения и видео. Оценочная система объединяет MLLM-ассистированные метрики с перцептивными и мультимодальными показателями качества, включая DINO-v2, ArcFace, CLIP и ImageBind, анализируя более 20 аспектов: качество сегментов, консистентность между сегментами, связность повествования и синхронизацию звука и видео. Экспериментальная валидация на 11 моделях с проверкой согласованности с человеческими оценками позволяет диагностировать ограничения современных систем генерации долгоформатного аудиовизуального контента.

en.title: Evaluating Minute-Long Audio-Visual Generation with LongAV-Compass
en.desc: LongAV-Compass is a new benchmark designed to evaluate the quality of minute-long audio-visual generation across different types of media. It addresses the limitations of existing benchmarks that focus mainly on short clips and lack comprehensive evaluation methods for longer content. The benchmark includes a variety of test cases that assess how well generated audio and video maintain consistency and coherence over time. By integrating advanced metrics and human validation, LongAV-Compass helps identify the strengths and weaknesses of current audio-visual generation models.

zh.title: 分钟级音视频生成的新基准
zh.desc: LongAV-Compass是一个全面的基准，用于评估多模态的分钟级音视频生成，重点在于质量、一致性和时间序列的对齐。现有的评估协议主要集中在短视频生成，而LongAV-Compass填补了这一空白，支持文本、图像和视频的统一评估。该基准包含284个经过精心策划的测试案例，涵盖了文本到音视频、图像到音视频和视频到音视频的生成。通过结合多种评估指标，LongAV-Compass为分析当前系统在生成一致、语义对齐和时间一致的分钟级音视频内容方面的局限性提供了实验平台。

papers.title: Does Seeing More Mean Knowing More? Mono-Anchored Advantage Normalization for Multi-Source Visual Reasoning
papers.abstract: Abstract A novel mono-anchored multi-source reasoning framework that uses dynamic anchors to quantify information gain and regulate modality interactions during reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards.  AI-generated summary Visual reasoning through reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) has achieved remarkable progress. However, when dealing with multi-source inputs, existing approaches tend to treat them as a mere accumulation of information, lacking explicit mechanisms to distinguish whether integrating additional sources yields information gain or introduces interference. Therefore, they struggle to effectively model dynamic interaction when integrating multiple sources, particularly when they differ significantly in physical properties and semantics, e.g., infrared and depth, leading to inferior performance to mono-source reasoning when a certain source holds the dominant signal. To address this issue, we propose MARS, a novel mono-anchored multi-source reasoning framework that models each visual modality as an independent information source. Specifically, by treating mono-source rewards as dynamic anchors, our method explicitly incorporates the information gain introduced by multi-source fusion into advantage normalization and adaptively emphasizes mutual promotion between sources while suppressing potential noise or conflicts during RLVR. From theoretical analysis, our method effectively quantifies information gain introduced by multi-source integration in gradient estimation, enabling consistent modality regulation. Empirical results also show impressive 3.2% and 4.9% performance gains on GRPO and DAPO across diverse datasets, confirming effectiveness of our method.

ru.title: Динамическая регуляция мультимодальности через информационный выигрыш в обучении с подкреплением
ru.desc: Статья представляет MARS — новую структуру для многомодального визуального рассуждения, которая использует динамические якори в виде вознаграждений одного модального источника для количественной оценки выигрыша информации. Метод явно моделирует взаимодействие между различными источниками данных (например, инфракрасное и глубинное видение), избегая их простого накопления и подавляя потенциальные конфликты и шумы. Авторы модифицируют нормализацию преимущества в обучении с подкреплением, чтобы адаптивно регулировать вклад каждой модальности в зависимости от информационного прироста. Экспериментальные результаты демонстрируют значительное улучшение производительности на 3,2% и 4,9% на различных датасетах, подтверждая эффективность предложенного подхода.

en.title: Enhancing Multi-Source Reasoning with Dynamic Anchors in RL
en.desc: This paper introduces MARS, a new framework for multi-source reasoning in reinforcement learning that uses dynamic anchors to enhance information processing. It addresses the challenge of integrating multiple data sources, such as infrared and depth images, by quantifying information gain and managing interactions between different modalities. The framework treats each visual modality as an independent source, allowing for better regulation of information flow and reducing interference. Empirical results demonstrate significant performance improvements, showcasing the effectiveness of this approach in visual reasoning tasks.

zh.title: 动态锚点驱动的多源推理新框架
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种新颖的单锚多源推理框架MARS，旨在通过动态锚点量化信息增益，并在强化学习中调节不同模态的交互。现有方法在处理多源输入时，往往将其视为信息的简单累积，缺乏有效区分信息增益和干扰的机制。MARS将每种视觉模态视为独立的信息源，通过将单源奖励视为动态锚点，显式地将多源融合带来的信息增益纳入优势归一化中。实验结果表明，该方法在多个数据集上实现了显著的性能提升，验证了其有效性。

papers.title: Soap2Soap: Long Cinematic Video Remaking via Multi-Agent Collaboration
papers.abstract: Abstract A multi-agent framework called Soap2Soap is presented for long-horizon video-to-video generation that maintains narrative structure and character identity across extended sequences through consistent semantic backbone and visual reference anchors.  AI-generated summary We study series-level cinematic remaking, a long-horizon video-to-video generation problem that localizes full episodes or films via stylization or actor replacement while strictly preserving narrative structure, motion choreography, and character identity across hundreds of shots. Existing video generation and editing pipelines often break down in this regime due to compounding identity drift, background mutation, and semantic erosion under large camera motions and viewpoint changes. We propose Soap2Soap, a multi-agent framework that enforces long-term language-visual consistency through a Dual-Bridge Consistency mechanism: a scene-aware JSON screenplay serving as a persistent semantic backbone, and dynamically allocated visual reference anchors at both scene and shot levels. To suppress drift before video synthesis, we introduce batch keyframe consistency, jointly generating multiple keyframes in a shared latent context via a grid-based formulation. A closed-loop verification agent further audits identity, stability, and alignment to trigger selective regeneration. Experiments on SoapBench demonstrate strong improvements over commercial video generation APIs in long-term consistency and narrative fidelity.
affiliations: Show Lab, National University of Singapore

ru.title: Сохранение идентичности в долгосрочной трансформации видео через многоагентную синхронизацию
ru.desc: Статья представляет Soap2Soap — многоагентную систему для долгосрочной генерации видео на основе другого видео, которая сохраняет нарративную структуру и идентичность персонажей на протяжении сотен кадров. Основной вклад работы — механизм двойной консистентности, где JSON-сценарий служит семантическим остовом, а визуальные якоря динамически распределяются на уровне сцен и отдельных кадров. Для предотвращения дрейфа идентичности перед синтезом видео применяется групповая консистентность ключевых кадров через сетевую формулировку в общем латентном пространстве. Замкнутый верификационный агент проверяет идентичность, стабильность и соответствие нарративу, запуская избирательную регенерацию проблемных фрагментов.

en.title: Soap2Soap: Consistent Video Generation Across Long Narratives
en.desc: The paper introduces Soap2Soap, a multi-agent framework designed for generating long videos while keeping the story and characters consistent. It addresses challenges like identity drift and background changes that often occur in video generation. The framework uses a Dual-Bridge Consistency mechanism, which includes a JSON screenplay for maintaining semantic structure and visual anchors for reference. Additionally, it employs batch keyframe consistency and a verification agent to ensure the generated videos maintain high quality and narrative integrity.

zh.title: 长时间视频生成的新突破：Soap2Soap框架
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种名为Soap2Soap的多智能体框架，用于长时间的视频生成，能够在扩展序列中保持叙事结构和角色身份的一致性。该框架通过双桥一致性机制，利用场景感知的JSON剧本作为持久的语义基础，并在场景和镜头层面动态分配视觉参考锚点。为了在视频合成前抑制身份漂移，本文引入了批量关键帧一致性，通过基于网格的形式共同生成多个关键帧。实验结果表明，Soap2Soap在长期一致性和叙事保真度方面显著优于现有的商业视频生成API。

papers.title: SpatialBench: Is Your Spatial Foundation Model an All-Round Player?
papers.abstract: Abstract SpatialBench presents a comprehensive benchmark for evaluating spatial foundation models across diverse domains and tasks, revealing limitations in current models and introducing DA-Next-5M and DA-Next to advance spatial representation learning.  AI-generated summary While spatial foundation models have demonstrated impressive performance on standard datasets, a critical question remains: are they truly all-round players capable of generalizing robustly across diverse downstream tasks, arbitrary viewpoints, shifting scene domains, varying input densities, and specific hardware constraints? Answering this overarching question requires a holistic assessment, yet current models are mainly evaluated on specific domains for which they were specifically designed or trained. Such evaluations are intrinsically limited by narrow paradigm coverage, limited scene domains, and arbitrary frame sampling, making it fundamentally difficult to assess their true generalization capabilities. To address this gap, we present SpatialBench, a cross-paradigm, domain-diverse benchmark for spatial foundation models with deterministic sampling. SpatialBench features unprecedented scale and rigorous deterministic design, comprising 19 datasets and 546 scenes across 5 diverse spatial domains. It comprehensively evaluates 41 models across 6 paradigms on 5 task suites under 4 different input density settings. Our extensive evaluation reveals that current models are not yet all-round players, and uncovers crucial insights for future advancement. Specifically, we demonstrate that full-context attention maximizes accuracy while bounded-memory strategies unlock long-sequence scalability. Moreover, our empirical evaluations in challenging embodied and egocentric tasks demonstrate that strict domain alignment and high data quality are far more critical to performance than simple dataset scaling. Furthermore, to address the largest data gap identified in our analysis, we go beyond evaluation by introducing a large-scale dataset, DA-Next-5M, and a strong baseline model, DA-Next, pushing the boundaries of spatial representation learning.
affiliations: Hong Kong University of Science and Technology
Huazhong University of Science and Technology

ru.title: Универсальные пространственные модели: от оценки к практическому решению
ru.desc: SpatialBench — это комплексный бенчмарк для оценки пространственных фундаментальных моделей на разнообразных задачах и доменах. Авторы выявили, что существующие модели не являются универсальными и плохо обобщаются при изменении точек зрения, доменов сцен и плотности входных данных. Исследование охватило 41 модель, 6 парадигм и 19 датасетов, позволив идентифицировать ключевые ограничения современных подходов. Для преодоления выявленных проблем авторы предложили новый датасет DA-Next-5M и базовую модель DA-Next, которые значительно улучшают качество обучения пространственного представления.

en.title: Benchmarking Spatial Models for Better Generalization
en.desc: SpatialBench is a new benchmark designed to evaluate spatial foundation models across various tasks and domains. It highlights the limitations of current models, showing that they struggle to generalize well across different scenarios and input conditions. The benchmark includes 19 datasets and 546 scenes, rigorously testing 41 models under diverse settings. Additionally, it introduces DA-Next-5M and DA-Next to enhance spatial representation learning, emphasizing the importance of data quality and context in improving model performance.

zh.title: 推动空间表示学习的全面基准测试
zh.desc: SpatialBench是一个全面的基准测试，用于评估空间基础模型在不同领域和任务中的表现。该研究揭示了当前模型的局限性，并引入了DA-Next-5M和DA-Next，以推动空间表示学习的发展。通过对41个模型在5个任务套件下的评估，研究发现现有模型尚未具备全面的通用能力。研究还强调了全上下文注意力和有限内存策略在提高准确性和扩展性方面的重要性。

papers.title: Efficient Agentic Reinforcement Learning with On-Policy Intrinsic Knowledge Boundary Enhancement
papers.abstract: Abstract AKBE enhances LLM agent training by dynamically identifying when tools are needed versus when internal knowledge suffices, improving accuracy and reducing unnecessary tool usage through targeted supervisory signals.  AI-generated summary Agentic reinforcement learning (RL) has proven effective for training LLM-based agents with external tool-use capabilities. However, we identify that agentic RL training induces increasing redundant tool calls and blurs the model's intrinsic knowledge boundary, where the model fails to distinguish when tools are needed versus when parametric knowledge suffices. Existing solutions based on reward shaping create coarse-grained optimization targets that tend to incentivize indiscriminate tool-call suppression, leading to reward hacking. In this paper, we propose AKBE (Agentic Knowledge Boundary Enhancement), an on-policy method that dynamically probes the model's intrinsic knowledge boundary through dual-path (with-tool and no-tool) rollouts during training. We define the knowledge boundary as the per-instance determination of whether tools are required and the minimum tool calls necessary. By comparing correctness across paths, AKBE categorizes trajectories and constructs targeted supervisory signals that guide efficient tool-use patterns for each question. These signals are integrated seamlessly into the agentic RL training loop. Experiments on seven QA benchmarks demonstrate that AKBE improves task accuracy by +1.85 on average and reduces tool calls by 18% over standard agentic RL, yielding 25% higher tool productivity without any accuracy-efficiency trade-off. Further analysis suggests its plug-and-play compatibility across different RL algorithms and the mechanism of each signal category. Our code is available at https://github.com/CuSO4-Chen/AKBE.
affiliations: The Chinese University of Hong Kong Shenzhen

ru.title: Умное разграничение: когда полагаться на знания модели, а когда использовать инструменты
ru.desc: В статье предлагается метод AKBE для улучшения обучения LLM-агентов с помощью обучения с подкреплением. Основная проблема заключается в том, что во время обучения агентов модель начинает избыточно использовать внешние инструменты и теряет способность различать, когда нужны инструменты, а когда достаточно собственных знаний. AKBE решает эту проблему через двусторонние примеры траекторий (с инструментами и без них), которые позволяют динамически определить границу между внутренними знаниями и необходимостью использования инструментов. Метод генерирует целевые сигналы обучения для каждого примера, что повышает точность на 1.85% и снижает количество вызовов инструментов на 18% без потери качества.

en.title: Optimizing Tool Use in LLM Training with AKBE
en.desc: The paper introduces AKBE, a method that enhances the training of large language model (LLM) agents by effectively determining when to use external tools versus relying on internal knowledge. It addresses the issue of redundant tool usage in agentic reinforcement learning (RL) by dynamically probing the model's knowledge boundaries during training. By implementing dual-path rollouts, AKBE generates targeted supervisory signals that optimize tool usage for specific tasks. Experimental results show that AKBE improves accuracy and reduces unnecessary tool calls, leading to more efficient and productive agent performance.

zh.title: 动态识别工具需求，提升模型效率
zh.desc: AKBE（Agentic Knowledge Boundary Enhancement）是一种增强大语言模型（LLM）代理训练的方法。它通过动态识别何时需要工具以及何时可以依赖内部知识，来提高模型的准确性并减少不必要的工具使用。该方法通过双路径训练（有工具和无工具）来探测模型的知识边界，从而构建针对性的监督信号，指导高效的工具使用模式。实验结果表明，AKBE在七个问答基准上平均提高了1.85的任务准确率，并减少了18%的工具调用，显著提升了工具的生产力。

papers.title: LocateAnything: Fast and High-Quality Vision-Language Grounding with Parallel Box Decoding
papers.abstract: Abstract Parallel Box Decoding enables efficient and accurate unified visual grounding and detection by decoding geometric elements as atomic units, improving both throughput and localization quality.  AI-generated summary Vision-language models (VLMs) commonly formulate visual grounding and detection as a coordinate-token generation problem, serializing each 2D box into multiple 1D tokens that are learned and decoded largely independently. This token-by-token decoding mismatches the coupled structure of box geometry and creates a practical inference bottleneck due to strictly sequential generation. We introduce LocateAnything, a unified generative grounding and detection framework based on Parallel Box Decoding (PBD). By decoding geometric elements such as bounding boxes and points as atomic units in a single step, LocateAnything preserves intra-box geometric coherence and unlocks substantial parallelism. We show that PBD improves both decoding throughput and localization accuracy. We further develop a scalable data engine and curate LocateAnything-Data, a large-scale dataset with more than 138 million training samples, substantially increasing data diversity for high-precision localization. Extensive evaluations show that LocateAnything advances the speed-accuracy frontier, achieving significantly higher decoding throughput while improving high-IoU localization quality across diverse benchmarks. The results highlight the complementary benefits of Parallel Box Decoding and large-scale training data in enabling efficient and precise unified visual grounding and detection.

ru.title: Параллельное декодирование боксов для быстрой и точной визуальной локализации
ru.desc: В работе предлагается новый метод Parallel Box Decoding для одновременного решения задач визуального грунтирования и детекции объектов в vision-language моделях. Вместо традиционного последовательного декодирования координат бокса токен за токеном, авторы декодируют геометрические элементы как единые атомарные единицы в один шаг, что сохраняет внутреннюю согласованность геометрии и позволяет распараллелить вычисления. Для обучения модели LocateAnything авторы создали большой датасет из 138 миллионов примеров, значительно повысив разнообразие данных для точной локализации объектов. Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что предложенный подход одновременно улучшает пропускную способность декодирования и точность локализации на различных бенчмарках.

en.title: Unlocking Speed and Precision in Visual Grounding with Parallel Box Decoding
en.desc: This paper introduces Parallel Box Decoding (PBD), a method that enhances visual grounding and detection by treating geometric elements as atomic units. Traditional approaches serialize 2D bounding boxes into multiple 1D tokens, which leads to inefficiencies and inaccuracies due to their independent decoding. By using PBD, the framework called LocateAnything can decode bounding boxes and points in a single step, maintaining the geometric relationships and allowing for faster processing. The authors also present a large dataset, LocateAnything-Data, which supports the training of models with diverse examples, ultimately improving both speed and localization accuracy in visual tasks.

zh.title: 并行框解码：提升视觉定位与检测的效率与准确性
zh.desc: 本文介绍了一种名为LocateAnything的框架，利用并行框解码（PBD）来实现高效且准确的视觉定位和检测。通过将几何元素（如边界框和点）作为原子单元在一步中解码，PBD保持了框内几何的一致性，并解锁了显著的并行性。研究表明，PBD不仅提高了解码的吞吐量，还改善了定位的准确性。此外，本文还开发了一个可扩展的数据引擎，创建了包含超过1.38亿个训练样本的LocateAnything-Data数据集，显著增加了数据的多样性。

papers.title: The MiniMax-M2 Series: Mini Activations Unleashing Max Real-World Intelligence
papers.abstract: Abstract The MiniMax-M2 series introduces Mixture-of-Experts language models with minimal activated parameters that achieve high performance in agentic tasks through specialized training and deployment systems.  AI-generated summary We introduce the MiniMax-M2 series, a family of Mixture-of-Experts language models built around the principle that mini activations can unleash maximum real-world intelligence. The flagship M2 contains 229.9B total parameters with only 9.8B activated per token. Designed end-to-end for agentic deployment, the M2 series rests on three components: (i) agent-driven data pipelines producing large-scale, verifiable trajectories across agentic coding and agentic cowork, each grounded in an executable workspace and an artifact-aligned reward; (ii) Forge, a scalable agent-native RL system that adapts to long-horizon agent trajectories, paired with windowed-FIFO scheduling, prefix-tree merging, inference optimization, and a clean training-inference-agent decoupling that supports both white-box and black-box agents; (iii) the latest M2.7 checkpoint takes an early step toward self-evolution -- autonomously debugging training runs and modifying its own scaffold. Across M2 through M2.7, this combination translates a mini-activation footprint into frontier-tier performance on agentic coding, deep search, office-task, and reasoning benchmarks.

ru.title: Минимум активации — максимум интеллекта агентов
ru.desc: Представлена серия MiniMax-M2 — языковые модели с архитектурой Mixture-of-Experts, которые активируют только 9.8B параметров из 229.9B общего числа на каждом токене. Модели специально обучены для агентных задач с помощью трёхкомпонентной системы: агент-ориентированные пайплайны данных, система обучения с подкреплением Forge для длинных горизонтов агентов, и самоэволюция через автоматическую отладку. Эффективная архитектура с минимальной активацией параметров позволяет достичь передовых результатов в задачах агентного кодирования, глубокого поиска и рассуждений. Система демонстрирует, что правильный дизайн архитектуры и обучения может компенсировать меньший размер модели на практических приложениях.

en.title: Maximize Intelligence with Minimal Activation
en.desc: The MiniMax-M2 series presents a new approach to language models using Mixture-of-Experts, which allows for minimal parameter activation while maintaining high performance in complex tasks. The flagship model, M2, boasts 229.9 billion parameters but activates only 9.8 billion per token, optimizing efficiency. It incorporates advanced systems for data processing and reinforcement learning, enabling it to handle long-term tasks effectively. Additionally, the M2.7 version introduces self-evolution capabilities, allowing the model to autonomously improve its training processes.

zh.title: 最小激活，最大智能
zh.desc: MiniMax-M2系列引入了混合专家语言模型，这些模型通过最小化激活参数实现高效能，特别适用于智能任务。旗舰模型M2拥有2299亿个总参数，但每个token仅激活9.8亿个参数。该系列模型设计为端到端，包含三个主要组件：驱动数据管道的智能代理、可扩展的原生强化学习系统Forge，以及最新的M2.7检查点，具备自我进化能力。通过这种组合，MiniMax-M2系列在智能编码、深度搜索、办公任务和推理基准测试中展现出卓越的性能。

papers.title: RT-Lynx: Putting the GEMM Sparsity In a Right Way for Diffusion Models
papers.abstract: Abstract Diffusion Transformers achieve strong image generation performance but face high inference costs; this work proposes RT-Lynx, which uses activation sparsification and optimized CUDA kernels to accelerate inference while maintaining generation quality.  AI-generated summary Diffusion Transformers (DiT) achieve strong performance in image generation but incur substantial inference costs. While prior work has reduced this cost via quantization and distillation, semi-structured sparsity, which can nearly halve FLOPs, remains underexplored. A key reason is that most existing approaches focus on weight sparsification, and pruning 50% of the weights can remove critical model capacity and degrade generation quality. Our study, however, shows that DiT activations are intrinsically sparse and significantly more robust to N:M semi-structured sparsification than weights. Motivated by this observation, we advocate a paradigm shift from weight sparsification to activation sparsification. We propose RT-Lynx, which applies N:M sparsification to activations and incorporates error-compensation techniques to mitigate accuracy loss. We further implement highly optimized CUDA kernels tailored to this setting, achieving up to a 1.55x speedup on average in linear layers. Extensive experiments across multiple diffusion models demonstrate that our method preserves the generation quality of the original models while substantially accelerating inference.

ru.title: От разреживания весов к разреживанию активаций: быстрые диффузионные трансформеры
ru.desc: В работе предлагается RT-Lynx — метод ускорения инференса диффузионных трансформеров через разреживание активаций вместо весов модели. Авторы показывают, что активации диффузионных моделей по своей природе содержат много нулевых значений и гораздо устойчивее к N:M полуструктурированному разреживанию, чем веса. Метод включает технику компенсации ошибок для сохранения качества генерации при разреживании активаций, а также оптимизированные CUDA ядра для эффективного вычисления на GPU. Экспериментальные результаты показывают ускорение инференса до 1.55x без заметного снижения качества генерируемых изображений.

en.title: Accelerating Image Generation with Activation Sparsification
en.desc: This paper introduces RT-Lynx, a method designed to enhance the efficiency of Diffusion Transformers in image generation by focusing on activation sparsification instead of traditional weight sparsification. The authors highlight that activations in these models are naturally sparse and can withstand semi-structured sparsification without significantly degrading performance. By implementing optimized CUDA kernels, RT-Lynx achieves a notable speedup in inference times while maintaining the quality of generated images. The results show that this approach can nearly halve the computational load, making it a promising solution for faster image generation in machine learning applications.

zh.title: 加速推理，保持生成质量的RT-Lynx
zh.desc: 本研究提出了一种名为RT-Lynx的方法，旨在加速扩散变换器（Diffusion Transformers）的推理过程，同时保持生成质量。通过对激活进行稀疏化和优化CUDA内核，RT-Lynx能够显著降低推理成本。与传统的权重稀疏化方法不同，我们发现激活本身具有内在的稀疏性，并且对N:M半结构稀疏化更具鲁棒性。实验结果表明，RT-Lynx在多个扩散模型上实现了推理速度的提升，同时保持了生成质量。

papers.title: Squeezing Capacity from Multimodal Large Language Models for Subject-driven Generation
papers.abstract: Abstract A novel approach conditions diffusion models on multimodal large language models for subject-driven image generation, combining text and reference image encoding with VAE-based identity conditioning to improve both semantic understanding and identity preservation.  AI-generated summary Subject-driven image generation aims to synthesize new images that preserve the identity of the given subject while following textual instructions. Existing approaches often encode text and reference images separately. This limits cross-modal reasoning abilities and causes copy-paste artifacts. Recent frameworks that connect multimodal models and diffusion models improve instruction following, but largely overlook identity preservation. To address these limitations, we condition diffusion models on Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) that jointly encode text and reference images, and augment it with VAE-based identity conditioning. A novel Dual Layer Aggregation (DLA) module is designed to aggregate multi-level MLLM features for optimal conditioning, and a multi-stage denoising strategy is applied to progressively balance the semantic information from MLLM and fine-detail identity from VAE during inference. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our approach harmonizes multimodal understanding with identity preservation, mitigates copy-paste issues, and achieves superior performance regarding human preference on subject-driven image generation. Our project website is available at https://zsh2000.github.io/squeeze-mllm-subject-gen/.

ru.title: Гармония текста и идентичности: диффузионные модели с многомодальным пониманием
ru.desc: В работе предложен новый подход для генерации изображений, управляемых объектом, путём кондиционирования диффузионных моделей на многомодальных больших языковых моделях (MLLM). Метод совместно кодирует текстовые инструкции и эталонные изображения, что улучшает кросс-модальное рассуждение и избегает артефактов копирования-вставки. Дополнительное кондиционирование на основе VAE сохраняет идентичность объекта, а специально разработанный модуль Dual Layer Aggregation эффективно агрегирует многоуровневые признаки MLLM. Многоэтапная стратегия денойзирования во время инференса гармонично балансирует семантическую информацию и детали идентичности, достигая превосходных результатов по предпочтениям людей.

en.title: Harmonizing Identity and Instruction in Image Generation
en.desc: This paper presents a new method for generating images based on specific subjects while maintaining their identity and following text instructions. It combines the strengths of diffusion models with multimodal large language models (MLLMs) to encode both text and reference images together, enhancing cross-modal reasoning. The approach introduces a Dual Layer Aggregation (DLA) module to optimize feature aggregation and employs a multi-stage denoising strategy to balance semantic understanding and identity details. Experiments show that this method effectively reduces copy-paste artifacts and improves overall image generation quality based on human preferences.

zh.title: 多模态大语言模型助力主题驱动图像生成
zh.desc: 这篇论文提出了一种新方法，将扩散模型与多模态大语言模型结合，用于基于主题的图像生成。该方法通过联合编码文本和参考图像，并使用基于变分自编码器的身份条件，来提高语义理解和身份保留能力。与现有方法不同，这种方法解决了跨模态推理能力不足和复制粘贴伪影的问题。实验结果表明，该方法在保持身份的同时，增强了多模态理解，显著提升了人类偏好的图像生成效果。

papers.title: MRT: Masked Region Transformer for Layered Image Generation and Editing at Scale
papers.abstract: Abstract A 20B-parameter masked region diffusion model enables scalable multi-layer transparent image generation and editing through unified task handling and efficient canvas management.  AI-generated summary Layered image generation and editing is a fundamental capability that enables layer-wise reuse, editing, and composition of generated visual content, analogous to word-level editing in natural language. Despite its importance, this remains an underexplored area at scale. To address this gap, we present MRT, a 20B-parameter masked region diffusion model tailored for multi-layer transparent image generation and editing, trained on over 10M multilingual design samples spanning diverse aspect ratios and textual prompts. To fully leverage this scale, we make two key technical contributions. First, we unify three complementary tasks including text-to-layers, image-to-layers, and layers-to-layers within a shared masked region diffusion framework, where selective token masking enables flexible layer-wise generation and editing. Second, to enable overflow layer generation, we introduce an overflow-aware canvas layer that handles boundary inconsistencies and supports semi-transparent background synthesis, enabling complete editable layers extending beyond visible canvas boundaries. Additionally, we apply diffusion distillation to achieve 8-step, real-time multi-layer generation with minimal quality degradation. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our framework substantially outperforms prior state-of-the-art approaches, including various commercial systems, across all three tasks, establishing a new benchmark for multi-layer transparent image generation. Notably, our model significantly outperforms the concurrent Qwen-Image-Layered model in image-to-layers quality according to user-study results, while achieving 10-100\times faster inference and reducing activation GPU memory consumption by 50-90\% during image-to-layer inference.

ru.title: Единая диффузионная модель для масштабируемого многослойного редактирования прозрачных изображений
ru.desc: MRT — это диффузионная модель с 20 миллиардами параметров, предназначенная для генерации и редактирования многослойных прозрачных изображений. Модель объединяет три задачи (текст-в-слои, изображение-в-слои, слои-в-слои) в единую архитектуру на основе маскированной диффузии с селективным маскированием токенов для гибкого редактирования. Авторы введли слой холста, осведомленный о переполнении, который корректно обрабатывает граничные артефакты и полупрозрачные фоны, позволяя создавать редактируемые слои за границами видимого холста. С помощью дистилляции диффузии модель достигает генерации в реальном времени за 8 шагов, превосходя аналогичные системы в качестве и скорости вычислений.

en.title: Revolutionizing Multi-Layer Image Generation with MRT
en.desc: This paper introduces MRT, a 20B-parameter masked region diffusion model designed for scalable multi-layer transparent image generation and editing. It unifies three tasks—text-to-layers, image-to-layers, and layers-to-layers—using a shared framework that allows for flexible layer-wise manipulation through selective token masking. The model also features an overflow-aware canvas layer to manage boundary issues and support semi-transparent backgrounds, enhancing the editing capabilities. Extensive experiments show that MRT significantly outperforms existing methods in speed and quality, setting a new standard for multi-layer image generation.

zh.title: 多层透明图像生成的新标杆
zh.desc: 本文介绍了一种名为MRT的20B参数的掩蔽区域扩散模型，专注于多层透明图像的生成和编辑。该模型通过统一处理文本到层、图像到层和层到层的任务，实现了灵活的层级生成和编辑。为了处理边界不一致性，模型引入了溢出感知画布层，支持半透明背景合成。实验结果表明，MRT在多层透明图像生成方面显著优于现有的最先进方法，设立了新的基准。

papers.title: Negligible in Size, Significant in Effect: On Scale Vectors in Large Language Models
papers.abstract: Abstract Scale vectors in LLMs significantly impact optimization despite minimal parameter count, with theoretical analysis and practical improvements showing enhanced training performance and scaling behavior.  AI-generated summary Normalization layers in modern large language models (LLMs) consist of a deterministic normalization operation and a learnable scale vector. While the normalization operation has been extensively studied, the scale vector remains poorly understood despite its ubiquitous use. In this work, we present a systematic study of scale vectors in LLMs from the perspectives of expressivity, optimization, and architectural structure. First, we show empirically that although scale vectors constitute only a negligible fraction of model parameters, removing them substantially degrades LLM pre-training. Our theory further shows that, in Pre-Norm architectures, scale vectors do not increase expressivity; instead, they improve optimization through a self-amplifying preconditioning effect on subsequent linear mappings. Second, we investigate the role of weight decay for scale vectors. By distinguishing Input-Norm and Output-Norm layers, we theoretically show that weight decay is beneficial for the former but harmful for the latter, due to their distinct roles in optimization and expressivity. Third, motivated by this understanding, we propose three lightweight and complementary improvements to scale vectors: branch-specific heterogeneity, improved placement around linear mappings, and magnitude-direction reparameterization. Both theory and experiments show that each improvement yields consistent gains. Finally, we combine these improvements into a unified scale-vector strategy and evaluate it through extensive LLM pre-training experiments on dense and mixture-of-experts models ranging from 0.12B to 2B parameters, across multiple optimizers and learning rate schedules, under industrial-scale token budgets. The unified strategy consistently achieves lower terminal loss than well-tuned baselines and exhibits more favorable scaling behavior, while adding negligible parameter and computational overhead.

ru.title: Маленькие векторы с большим влиянием: оптимизируем масштабирование в LLM
ru.desc: В этой работе исследуются векторы масштабирования в больших языковых моделях (LLM), которые являются компонентами слоёв нормализации. Авторы показывают теоретически и экспериментально, что хотя эти векторы составляют малую долю параметров модели, они существенно влияют на оптимизацию обучения благодаря эффекту предобусловливания. Исследование выявило, что регуляризация (weight decay) по-разному влияет на разные типы слоёв нормализации, и предложены три улучшения: ветвевая гетерогенность, лучшее размещение и переопараметризация. Предложенная стратегия продемонстрировала улучшение качества обучения и масштабируемости на моделях размером от 120 млн до 2 млрд параметров с минимальными вычислительными затратами.

en.title: Unlocking Optimization: The Power of Scale Vectors in LLMs
en.desc: This paper investigates the role of scale vectors in large language models (LLMs) and their impact on optimization and training performance. Despite being a small part of the model's parameters, the removal of scale vectors significantly harms pre-training effectiveness. The authors provide theoretical insights showing that in Pre-Norm architectures, scale vectors enhance optimization rather than expressivity by improving the conditioning of linear mappings. They also propose three enhancements to scale vectors, which collectively lead to better training outcomes without adding substantial computational costs.

zh.title: 优化大型语言模型的尺度向量策略
zh.desc: 在大型语言模型（LLMs）中，尺度向量对优化有显著影响，尽管其参数数量很少。本文系统研究了尺度向量的表达能力、优化和架构结构，发现去除尺度向量会显著降低预训练性能。我们提出了三种轻量级的改进方法，旨在优化尺度向量的使用，实验结果表明这些改进能有效提升模型性能。最终，我们将这些改进整合为统一的尺度向量策略，经过广泛的预训练实验验证了其优越性。

papers.title: MUSE-Autoskill: Self-Evolving Agents via Skill Creation, Memory, Management, and Evaluation
papers.abstract: Abstract A skill-centric agent framework enables continuous improvement of task-solving capabilities through a unified lifecycle of skill creation, memory, management, evaluation, and refinement.  AI-generated summary Large language model (LLM) agents rely on reusable skills to solve complex tasks. However, existing skill creation approaches treat skills as isolated and static artifacts, limiting their reusability, reliability, and long-term improvement. We propose MUSE-Autoskill Agent (Memory-Utilizing Skill Evolution), a skill-centric agent framework that lets agents continuously improve their task-solving capability by creating, reusing, and refining skills under a unified lifecycle (creation, memory, management, evaluation, and refinement). Our framework enables agents to create skills on demand, store and reuse them across tasks, organize and select them efficiently, and evaluate them through unit tests and runtime feedback for continuous refinement. We further introduce skill-level memory that accumulates experience for each skill across tasks, enabling more effective reuse and adaptation over time. Experiments on SkillsBench provide initial evidence that lifecycle-managed skills can improve task success, efficiency, reuse, and cross-agent transfer, highlighting the importance of treating skills as long-lived, experience-aware, and testable assets.

ru.title: Непрерывная эволюция переиспользуемых навыков агентов
ru.desc: В статье предлагается фреймворк MUSE-Autoskill Agent для управления навыками агентов на основе больших языковых моделей. Система позволяет агентам создавать, сохранять, переиспользовать и постоянно улучшать навыки через единый жизненный цикл, включающий создание, управление памятью, организацию, оценку и доработку. Фреймворк вводит память на уровне навыков, которая накапливает опыт каждого навыка при решении различных задач, обеспечивая более эффективное переиспользование и адаптацию. Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что управление жизненным циклом навыков улучшает успешность решения задач, эффективность работы и возможность передачи навыков между агентами.

en.title: Empowering AI with Evolving Skills for Continuous Improvement
en.desc: The paper introduces the MUSE-Autoskill Agent framework, which focuses on the continuous improvement of task-solving skills in AI agents. Unlike traditional methods that treat skills as static and isolated, this framework allows for the dynamic creation, management, and refinement of skills throughout their lifecycle. It emphasizes the importance of skill-level memory, which helps agents accumulate experience and adapt skills for better performance over time. Experimental results show that this approach enhances task success and efficiency, demonstrating the value of viewing skills as long-term, adaptable resources.

zh.title: 技能驱动，持续进步的智能代理框架
zh.desc: 本论文提出了一种以技能为中心的代理框架，旨在通过统一的技能生命周期（创建、记忆、管理、评估和改进）来持续提升任务解决能力。现有的技能创建方法将技能视为孤立和静态的产物，限制了其可重用性和长期改进。我们提出的MUSE-Autoskill代理框架允许代理根据需求创建、存储和重用技能，并通过单元测试和运行时反馈进行评估和持续改进。实验结果表明，生命周期管理的技能能够提高任务成功率、效率和跨代理转移，强调了将技能视为长期存在、经验丰富和可测试资产的重要性。
